text: According to Snowflake best practice recommendations, which role should be used to create databases?

options.a: ACCOUNTADMIN
options.c: SECURITYADMIN
options.d: USERADMIN

text: To add or remove search optimization for a table, a user must have which of the following privileges or roles? (Choose two.)

options.a: The MODIFY privilege on the table
options.b: The OWNERSHIP privilege on the table
options.c: A SECURITYADMIN role
options.d: The ADD SEARCH OPTIMIZATION privilege on the schema that contains the table
options.e: The SELECT privilege on the table

text: While using a COPY command with a Validation_mode parameter, which of the following statements will return an error?

options.a: Statements that insert a duplicate record during a load
options.b: Statements that have a specific data type in the source
options.c: Statements that have duplicate file names
options.d: Statements that transform data during a load

text: When is the result set cache no longer available? (Choose two.)

options.a: When another warehouse is used to execute the query
options.b: When another user executes the query
options.c: When the underlying data has changed
options.d: When the warehouse used to execute the query is suspended
options.e: When it has been 24 hours since the last query

text: What is the recommended file sizing for data loading using Snowpipe?

options.a: A compressed file size greater than 100 MB, and up to 250 MB
options.b: A compressed file size greater than 100 GB, and up to 250 GB
options.c: A compressed file size greater than 10 MB, and up to 100 MB
options.d: A compressed file size greater than 1 GB, and up to 2 GB

text: Which statements are true concerning Snowflake’s underlying cloud infrastructure? (Choose three.)

options.a: Snowflake data and services are deployed in a single availability zone within a cloud provider’s region.
options.b: Snowflake data and services are available in a single cloud provider and a single region; the use of multiple cloud providers is not supported.
options.c: Snowflake can be deployed in a customer’s private cloud using the customer’s own compute and storage resources.
options.d: Snowflake uses the core compute and storage services of each cloud provider for its own compute and storage.
options.e: All three layers of Snowflake’s architecture (storage, compute, and cloud services) are deployed and managed entirely on a selected cloud platform.
options.f: Snowflake data and services are deployed in at least three availability zones within a cloud provider’s region.

text: A user unloaded a Snowflake table called mytable to an internal stage called mystage. Which command can be used to view the list of files that has been uploaded to the stage?

options.a: list @mytable;
options.c: list @%mystage;
options.d: list @mystage;

text: What is a best practice after creating a custom role?

options.a: Create the custom role using the SYSADMIN role.
options.b: Assign the custom role to the SYSADMIN role.
options.c: Assign the custom role to the PUBLIC role.
options.d: Add _CUSTOM to all custom role names.

text: Which is the MINIMUM required Snowflake edition that a user must have if they want to use AWS/Azure Privatelink or Google Cloud Private Service Connect?

options.a: Standard
options.c: Enterprise
options.d: Business Critical

text: Which of the following query profiler variables will indicate that a virtual warehouse is not sized correctly for the query being executed?

options.a: Bytes sent over the network
options.c: Initialization
options.d: Remote spillage

text: Which of the following Snowflake capabilities are available in all Snowflake editions? (Choose two.)

options.a: Customer-managed encryption keys through Tri-Secret Secure
options.c: Up to 90 days of data recovery through Time Travel
options.d: Object-level access control
options.e: Column-level security to apply data masking policies to tables and views

text: A PUT command can be used to stage local files from which Snowflake interface?

options.a: SnowSQL
options.c: Snowsight
options.d: .NET driver

text: Which of the following indicates that it may be appropriate to use a clustering key for a table? (Choose two.)

options.a: The table contains a column that has very low cardinality.
options.b: DML statements that are being issued against the table are blocked.
options.c: The table has a small number of micro-partitions.
options.d: Queries on the table are running slower than expected.
options.e: The clustering depth for the table is large.

text: Which cache type is used to cache data output from SQL queries?

options.a: Metadata cache
options.c: Remote cache
options.d: Local file cache

text: Which of the following describes how clustering keys work in Snowflake?

options.a: Clustering keys update the micro-partitions in place with a full sort, and impact the DML operations.
options.b: Clustering keys sort the designated columns over time, without blocking DML operations.
options.c: Clustering keys create a distributed, parallel data structure of pointers to a table's rows and columns.
options.d: Clustering keys establish a hashed key on each node of a virtual warehouse to optimize joins at run-time.

text: Which of the following operations require the use of a running virtual warehouse? (Choose two.)

options.a: Downloading data from an internal stage
options.c: Executing a stored procedure
options.d: Altering a table
options.e: Querying data from a materialized view

text: What is used to limit the credit usage of a virtual warehouse within a Snowflake account?

options.a: Load monitor
options.c: Query Profile
options.d: Stream

text: What are the benefits of the replication feature in Snowflake? (Choose two.)

options.a: Disaster recovery
options.c: Fail-safe
options.d: Database failover and failback

text: Which of the following roles are recommended to create and manage users and roles? (Choose two.)

options.a: SYSADMIN
options.c: PUBLIC
options.d: ACCOUNTADMIN

text: What actions will prevent leveraging of the ResultSet cache?

options.a: Removing a column from the query SELECT list
options.b: Stopping the virtual warehouse that the query is running against
options.c: If the result has not been reused within the last 12 hours
options.d: Executing the RESULTS_SCAN() table function

text: Which of the following are benefits of micro-partitioning? (Choose two.)

options.a: Micro-partitions cannot overlap in their range of values.
options.b: Micro-partitions are immutable objects that support the use of Time Travel.
options.c: Micro-partitions can reduce the amount of I/O from object storage to virtual warehouses.
options.d: Rows are automatically stored in sorted order within micro-partitions.
options.e: Micro-partitions can be defined on a schema-by-schema basis.

text: Which data type can be used to store geospatial data in Snowflake?

options.a: Variant
options.c: Geometry
options.d: Geography

text: If all virtual warehouse resources are maximized while processing a query workload, what will happen to new queries that are submitted to the warehouse?

options.a: All queries will terminate when the resources are maximized.
options.b: The warehouse will scale out automatically
options.c: The warehouse will move to a suspended state.
options.d: New queries will be queued and executed when capacity is available.

text: Masking policies can be applied to which of the following Snowflake objects? (Choose two.)

options.a: A materialized view
options.c: A table
options.d: A stream

text: What actions are supported by Snowflake resource monitors? (Choose two.)

options.a: Alert
options.c: Notify and suspend
options.d: Abort

text: A user executes the following SQL query: create table SALES_BKP like SALES; What are the cost implications for processing this query?

options.a: Processing costs will be generated based on how long the query takes.
options.b: Storage costs will be generated based on the size of the data.
options.c: No costs will be incurred as the query will use metadata.
options.d: The cost for running the virtual warehouse will be charged by the second.

text: What is the maximum length of time travel available in the Snowflake Standard Edition?

options.a: 1 Day
options.c: 30 Days
options.d: 90 Days

text: What happens when an external or an internal stage is dropped? (Choose two.)

options.a: When dropping an external stage, the files are not removed and only the stage is dropped.
options.b: When dropping an external stage, both the stage and the files within the stage are removed.
options.c: When dropping an internal stage, the files are deleted with the stage and the files are recoverable.
options.d: When dropping an internal stage, the files are deleted with the stage and the files are not recoverable.
options.e: When dropping an internal stage, only selected files are deleted with the stage and are not recoverable.

text: A user has 10 files in a stage containing new customer data. The ingest operation completes with no errors, using the command: COPY INTO my_table FROM @my_stage; The next day the user adds 10 files so the stage contains a mixture of new data and updates. If the user runs the same COPY INTO command, what will happen?

options.a: All data from all files on the stage will be appended to the table.
options.b: Only data about new customers from the new files will be appended.
options.c: The operation will fail with error UNCERTAIN FILES IN STAGE.
options.d: All data from only the newly-added files will be appended to the table.

text: Which parameter can be used to instruct a COPY command to verify data files instead of loading them into a specified table?

options.a: STRIP_NULL_VALUES
options.c: REPLACE_INVALID_CHARACTERS
options.d: VALIDATION_MODE

text: Which of the following SQL statements will list the version of the drivers currently being used?

options.a: Execute SELECT CURRENT_ODBC_CLIENT(); from the Web UI
options.c: Execute SELECT CURRENT_CLIENT(); from an application
options.d: Execute SELECT CURRENT_VERSION(); from the Python Connector

text: Which Snowflake technique can be used to improve the performance of a query?

options.a: Clustering
options.c: Fragmenting
options.d: Using INDEX_HINTS

text: What happens to the shared objects for users in a consumer account from a share, once a database has been created in that account?

options.a: The shared objects are transferred.
options.c: The shared objects become accessible.
options.d: The shared objects can be re-shared.

text: Using variables in Snowflake is denoted by using which SQL character?
Which commands should be used to grant the privilege allowing a role to select data from all current tables and any tables that will be created later in a schema? (Choose two.)

options.a: grant USAGE on all tables in schema DB1.SCHEMA to role MYROLE;
options.b: grant USAGE on future tables in schema DB1.SCHEMA to role MYROLE;
options.c: grant SELECT on all tables in schema DB1.SCHEMA to role MYROLE;
options.d: grant SELECT on future tables in schema DB1.SCHEMA to role MYROLE;
options.e: grant SELECT on all tables in database DB1 to role MYROLE;
options.f: grant SELECT on future tables in database DB1 to role MYROLE;

text: How can a user change which columns are referenced in a view?

options.a: Modify the columns in the underlying table
options.b: Use the ALTER VIEW command to update the view
options.c: Recreate the view with the required changes
options.d: Materialize the view to perform the changes

text: Which statement describes pruning?

options.a: The filtering or disregarding of micro-partitions that are not needed to return a query.
options.b: The return of micro-partitions values that overlap with each other to reduce a query's runtime.
options.c: A service that is handled by the Snowflake Cloud Services layer to optimize caching.
options.d: The ability to allow the result of a query to be accessed as if it were a table.

text: Which SQL command can be used to see the CREATE definition of a masking policy?

options.a: SHOW MASKING POLICIES
options.c: GET_DDL
options.d: LIST MASKING POLICIES

text: Which of the following is the Snowflake Account_Usage.Metering_History view used for?

options.a: Gathering the hourly credit usage for an account
options.b: Compiling an account's average cloud services cost over the previous month
options.c: Summarizing the throughput of Snowpipe costs for an account
options.d: Calculating the funds left on an account's contract

text: Query parsing and compilation occurs in which architecture layer of the Snowflake Cloud Data Platform?

options.a: Cloud services layer
options.c: Storage layer
options.d: Cloud agnostic layer

text: Which of the following Snowflake objects can be shared using a secure share? (Choose two.)

options.a: Materialized views
options.c: Procedures
options.d: Tables

text: What happens to the underlying table data when a CLUSTER BY clause is added to a Snowflake table?

options.a: Data is hashed by the cluster key to facilitate fast searches
options.b: Larger micro-partitions are created for common data values
options.c: Smaller micro-partitions are created for common data values
options.d: Data may be colocated by the cluster key within micro-partitions to improve pruning performance

text: Which of the following conditions must be met in order to return results from the results cache? (Choose two.)

options.a: The user has appropriate privileges on the objects associated with the query.
options.b: Micro-partitions have been reclustered since the query was last run.
options.c: The new query is run using the same virtual warehouse as previous query.
options.d: The query includes a User Defined Function (UDF).
options.e: The query has been run within 24 hours of the previously-run query.

text: Which statement about billing applies to Snowflake credits?

options.a: Credits are billed per-minute with a 60-minute minimum.
options.b: Credits are used to pay for cloud data storage usage.
options.c: Credits are consumed based on the number of credits billed for each hour warehouse runs.
options.d: Credits are consumed based on the warehouse size and the time the warehouse is running.

text: A user needs to create a materialized view in schema MYDB.MYSCHEMA. Which statement will provide this access?

options.a: GRANT ROLE MYROLE TO USER USER1; CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW ON SCHEMA MYDB.MYSCHEMA TO ROLE MYROLE;
options.b: GRANT ROLE MYROLE TO USER USER1; CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW ON SCHEMA MYDB.MYSCHEMA TO USER USER1;
options.c: GRANT ROLE MYROLE TO USER USER1; CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW ON SCHEMA MYDB.MYSCHEMA TO USER1;
options.d: GRANT ROLE MYROLE TO USER USER1; CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW ON SCHEMA MYDB.MYSCHEMA TO MYROLE;

text: What is the purpose of multi-cluster virtual warehouses?

options.a: To create separate data warehouses to increase query optimization
options.b: To allow users to choose compute nodes that make up a cluster
options.c: To eliminate or reduce queuing of concurrent queries
options.d: To allow the warehouse to resize automatically

text: Which is a valid source for an external stage when the Snowflake account is located on Microsoft Azure?

options.a: An FTP server with TLS encryption
options.c: A Google Cloud storage bucket
options.d: A Windows server file share on Azure

text: Which database objects can be shared with the Snowflake secure data sharing feature? (Choose two.)

options.a: Files
options.c: Secure User-Defined Functions (UDFs)
options.d: Sequences

text: Which statements reflect key functionalities of a Snowflake Data Exchange? (Choose two.)

options.a: If account is enrolled with Data Exchange, it loses access to Marketplace.
options.b: A Data Exchange allows groups of accounts to share data privately.
options.c: A Data Exchange allows accounts to share data with third, non-Snowflake parties.
options.d: Data Exchange functionality is available by default in Enterprise edition or higher.
options.e: The sharing of data in a Data Exchange is bidirectional.

text: A Snowflake user executed a query and received results. Another user executed same query 4 hours later. Data had not changed. What will occur?

options.a: No virtual warehouse used, data read from result cache.
options.b: No virtual warehouse used, data read from local disk cache.
options.c: Default virtual warehouse used to read all data.
options.d: Virtual warehouse defined at session level used to read all data.

text: Which feature allows a user the ability to control the organization of data in a micro-partition?

options.a: Range Partitioning
options.c: Automatic Clustering
options.d: Horizontal Partitioning

text: Which privilege must be granted to a share to allow secure views the ability to reference data in multiple databases?

options.a: CREATE_SHARE on the account
options.c: SELECT on tables used by the secure view
options.d: REFERENCE_USAGE on databases

text: In which use case does Snowflake apply egress charges?

options.a: Data sharing within a specific region
options.c: Database replication
options.d: Loading data into Snowflake

text: Which of the following compute resources or features are managed by Snowflake? (Choose two.)

options.a: Execute a COPY command
options.c: Snowpipe
options.d: AUTOMATIC_CLUSTERING

text: A materialized view should be created when which of the following occurs? (Choose two.)

options.a: There is minimal cost associated with running the query.
options.b: The query consumes many compute resources every time it runs.
options.c: The base table gets updated frequently.
options.d: Query is highly optimized and doesn't consume many compute resources.
options.e: Results of query do not change often and are used frequently.

text: What privilege should a user be granted to change permissions for new objects in a managed access schema?

options.a: Grant the OWNERSHIP privilege on the schema.
options.b: Grant the OWNERSHIP privilege on the database.
options.c: Grant the MANAGE GRANTS global privilege.
options.d: Grant ALL privileges on the schema.

text: What happens when a Data Provider revokes privileges to a share on an object in their source database?

options.a: The object immediately becomes unavailable for all Data Consumers.
options.b: Any additional data arriving after this point in time will not be visible.
options.c: Consumers stop seeing updates and become responsible for storage.
options.d: Static copy of object at time privilege was revoked is created in Consumer account.

text: Which command can be used to load data into an internal stage?

options.a: LOAD
options.c: GET
options.d: PUT

text: What is the MINIMUM Snowflake edition required to use the periodic rekeying of micro-partitions?

options.a: Enterprise
options.c: Standard
options.d: Virtual Private Snowflake

text: Which stage type can be altered and dropped?

options.a: Database stage
options.c: Table stage
options.d: User stage

text: Which Snowflake object enables loading data from files as soon as they are available in cloud storage?

options.a: Pipe
options.c: Task
options.d: Stream

text: A user is loading JSON documents composed of a huge array into Snowflake. The user enables STRIP_OUTER_ARRAY option. What does it do?

options.a: It removes the last element of the outer array.
options.b: It removes outer array structure and loads records into separate rows.
options.c: It removes trailing spaces and loads records into separate columns.
options.d: It removes NULL elements and enables ability to load records.

text: Which describes how multiple Snowflake accounts in a single organization relate to various cloud providers?

options.a: Each Snowflake account can be hosted in a different cloud vendor and region.
options.b: Each Snowflake account must be hosted in a different vendor and region.
options.c: All Snowflake accounts must be hosted in same cloud vendor and region.
options.d: Each account can be in different cloud vendor, but must be same region.

text: If a Snowflake user decides a table should be clustered, what should be used as the cluster key?

options.a: The columns that are queried in the select clause.
options.b: The columns with very high cardinality.
options.c: The columns with many different values.
options.d: The columns most actively used in the select filters.

text: What are value types that a VARIANT column can store? (Choose two.)

options.a: STRUCT
options.c: BINARY
options.d: ARRAY

text: A company needs to read multiple terabytes of data for initial load. How does Snowflake recommend maximizing load performance?

options.a: Use auto-ingest Snowpipes to load large files in serverless model.
options.b: Produce largest files possible to reduce overall number of files.
options.c: Produce larger number of smaller files and process with size Small warehouses.
options.d: Use external tool to issue batched row-by-row inserts.

text: For non-materialized views, what column identifies whether a view is secure or not?

options.a: CHECK_OPTION
options.c: IS_UPDATEABLE
options.d: TABLE_NAME

text: The bulk data load history that is available upon completion of the COPY statement is stored where and for how long?

options.a: In the metadata of the target table for 14 days
options.b: In the metadata of the pipe for 14 days
options.c: In the metadata of the target table for 64 days
options.d: In the metadata of the pipe for 64 days

text: Which command will show all privileges granted to a schema?

options.a: SHOW GRANTS ON ROLE ROLE_NAME
options.c: SHOW GRANTS TO USER USER_NAME
options.d: SHOW GRANTS OF ROLE ROLE_NAME

text: Which of the following are characteristics of security in Snowflake?

options.a: Authentication is only available with Business Critical edition.
options.b: Support for HIPAA and GDPR compliance is available for UI editions.
options.c: Periodic rekeying of encrypted data is available with Enterprise edition and higher.
options.d: Private communication to internal stages is allowed in Enterprise edition.

text: Which of the following objects can be shared through secure data sharing?

options.a: Masking policy
options.c: Task
options.d: External table

text: Which formats does Snowflake store unstructured data in? (Choose two.)

options.a: GeoJSON
options.c: XML
options.d: Object

text: Which practice will provide the MOST efficient loading performance from external stage?

options.a: Organize files into logical paths
options.b: Store files on external stage to ensure caching is maintained
options.c: Use pattern matching for regular expression execution
options.d: Load the data in one large file

text: What effect does WAIT_FOR_COMPLETION = TRUE have when changing warehouse size?

options.a: Size doesn't change until running queries have completed.
options.b: Size doesn't change until queries in queue have completed.
options.c: Size doesn't change until warehouse is suspended and restarted.
options.d: It does not return from command until warehouse has finished changing size.

text: Which of the following can be used when unloading data from Snowflake? (Choose two.)

options.a: Unloading semi-structured, recommended to use STRIP_OUTER_ARRAY.
options.b: Use ENCODING option to change from default UTF-8.
options.c: OBJECT_CONSTRUCT function can convert relational data to semi-structured.
options.d: Using SINGLE = TRUE parameter, single file up to 5 GB can be exported.

text: What data is stored in the Snowflake storage layer? (Choose two.)

options.a: Snowflake parameters
options.c: Query history
options.d: Persisted query results

text: Based on a reader account configuration, what is true of the reader account?

options.a: The reader account will automatically use Standard edition.
options.b: The reader account compute will be billed to the provider account.
options.c: The reader account can clone data shared, but cannot re-share it.
options.d: The reader account can create copy using CREATE TABLE AS...

text: Which of the following activities consume virtual warehouse credits in the Snowflake environment? (Choose two.)

options.a: Caching query results
options.c: Cloning a database
options.d: Running a custom query

text: When loading data into Snowflake, the COPY command supports which of the following?

options.a: Joins
options.c: Column reordering
options.d: Aggregates

text: What is cached during a query on a virtual warehouse?

options.a: All columns in a micro-partition
options.b: Any columns accessed during the query
options.c: The columns in the result set of the query
options.d: All rows accessed during the query

text: What is the default character set used when loading CSV files into Snowflake?

options.a: UTF-8
options.d: ANSI_X3.4

text: Which of the following describes external functions in Snowflake?

options.a: They are a type of User-defined Function (UDF).
options.b: They contain their own SQL code.
options.c: They call code that is stored inside of Snowflake.
options.d: They can return multiple rows for each row received.

text: Which of the following are valid methods for authenticating users for access into Snowflake? (Choose three.)

options.a: SCIM
options.c: TLS 1.2
options.d: Key-pair authentication

text: Which condition will trigger a cluster to shut-down in a standard multi-cluster warehouse auto-scaling policy?

options.a: When after 2-3 consecutive checks the system determines that the load on the most-loaded cluster could be redistributed.
options.b: When after 5-6 consecutive checks the system determines that the load on the most-loaded cluster could be redistributed.
options.c: When after 5-6 consecutive checks the system determines that the load on the least-loaded cluster could be redistributed.
options.d: When after 2-3 consecutive checks the system determines that the load on the least-loaded cluster could be redistributed.

text: What is the minimum Snowflake edition needed for database failover and fail-back between Snowflake accounts for business continuity?

options.a: Standard
options.c: Business Critical
options.d: Virtual Private Snowflake

text: How would a user execute a series of SQL statements using a task?

options.a: Include the SQL statements in the body of the task separated by semicolons.
options.b: A task can only have one DML statement per invocation.
options.c: Use a stored procedure executing multiple SQL statements and invoke the stored procedure from the task.
options.d: Create a task for each SQL statement and string them together sequentially.

text: How many resource monitors can be assigned at the account level?
Data storage for individual tables can be monitored using which commands and/or objects? (Choose two.)

options.a: SHOW STORAGE BY TABLE;
options.c: Information Schema -> TABLE_HISTORY
options.d: Information Schema -> TABLE_FUNCTION

text: How would a user run a multi-cluster warehouse in maximized mode?

options.a: Configure the maximum clusters setting to 'Maximum'.
options.b: Turn on the additional clusters manually after starting the warehouse.
options.c: Set the minimum clusters and maximum clusters settings to the same value.
options.d: Set the minimum clusters and maximum clusters settings to different values.

text: What internal stages are available in Snowflake? (Choose three.)

options.a: Schema stage
options.c: User stage
options.d: Stream stage

text: Which stages are used with the Snowflake PUT command to upload files from a local file system? (Choose three.)

options.a: Schema Stage
options.c: Database Stage
options.d: Table Stage

text: Which data type can store more than one type of data structure?

options.a: JSON
options.c: VARCHAR
options.d: VARIANT

text: User-level network policies can be created by which of the following roles? (Choose two.)

options.a: ROLEADMIN
options.c: SYSADMIN
options.d: SECURITYADMIN

text: Which ACCOUNT_USAGE views are used to evaluate the details of dynamic data masking? (Choose two.)

options.a: ROLES
options.c: QUERY_HISTORY
options.d: RESOURCE_MONITORS

text: Which of the following are considerations when using a directory table when working with unstructured data?

options.a: Directory tables can only be used with internal stages.
options.b: Directory tables allow for cataloging and querying unstructured data files in cloud storage.
options.c: A warehouse is required to query the directory table.
options.d: Directory tables are a separate object and not part of a stage definition.

text: True or False: Snowflake supports the ingestion of data from on-premises stages directly using Snowpipe.

options.a: True

text: Which of the following statements is true regarding micro-partitions?

options.a: They allow for extremely large tables to be scanned efficiently through pruning.
options.b: Users must manually define the partition boundaries.
options.c: They are typically between 500 MB and 2 GB in size.
options.d: They only exist in the Business Critical edition.

text: What is the primary function of the Object Tagging feature?

options.a: To provide a way to group and track objects for governance and cost management.
options.b: To speed up query performance by tagging frequently used tables.
options.c: To encrypt sensitive data at the column level.
options.d: To share data with external partners.

text: True or False: A Snowflake account can be moved between cloud providers after it has been created.

options.a: True

text: Which Snowflake object is used to automate the execution of a Stored Procedure on a schedule?

options.a: Pipe
options.c: Stream
options.d: Function
